vocab.grammar: The sentence "Hello world." is a simple declarative/exclamatory phrase composed of two elements: (1) "Hello" — an interjection used as a greeting or to attract attention, carrying no grammatical subject or verb; (2) "world" — a singular common noun functioning as the object of address. The sentence lacks a main verb, making it a nominal or verbless utterance, which is typical of greetings and exclamations in English. The period at the end indicates a calm, declarative tone, though an exclamation mark (!) is also commonly used to express enthusiasm.

The sentence "Hello world." is a simple declarative/exclamatory phrase composed of two elements: (1) "Hello" — an interjection used as a greeting or to attract attention, carrying no grammatical subject or verb; (2) "world" — a singular common noun functioning as the object of address. The sentence lacks a main verb, making it a nominal or verbless utterance, which is typical of greetings and exclamations in English. The period at the end indicates a calm, declarative tone, though an exclamation mark (!) is also commonly used to express enthusiasm.
